INT. BOARDROOM - DAY

Twelve EXECUTIVES around a long table. The CEO stands.

                    CEO
          What we're looking at, gentlemen,
          is a complete restructuring of
          our operations in the Pacific.
          This includes, but is not limited
          to, the closure of three
          manufacturing plants, the
                              (MORE)

                                                       12.

                    CEO (CONT'D)
          consolidation of our supply
          chain, and the relocation of
          approximately four hundred
          employees.

A long silence. An EXECUTIVE, 50s, raises his hand.

                    EXECUTIVE
          And the timeline?

                    CEO
          Ninety days.

The room erupts. The CEO raises a hand for quiet.

                    CEO (CONT'D)
          I understand your concerns. But
          if we don't act now, there won't
          be a company to restructure. We
          have to be decisive, we have to
          be fast, and above all we have
          to be unified. Are we unified?
                              (MORE)

                                                       13.

                    CEO (CONT'D)
              (leaning forward)
          Are we unified?

Silence. Then, slowly, heads begin to nod.
